item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've made some slight alterations to the document, mainly in the interest of making it a bit more consistent with the site's tone and adding a few additional details.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rather boring, if I do say so myself, and a lot of the same thing… I'll have to downvote, especially since there's an SCP JUST like it in Series one, in the lower 100s if I remember correctly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I gave this +1, but shouldn't it be keter? If it gets out, humanity is defenceless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP-1026-B was located two miles from Sector ██"… We use metric. Think like this: the imperial system is used in only one or two countries while the rest of the world uses metric. Saying miles to someone who was raised using metric and never had the need or interest to learn imperial (like me) means as much as giving measures in biblical cubits. Use metric.
And it is not interesting enough for me to ignore one of my most hated tone issues, but still avoids a downvote.
No vote, but I liked the authopsy.

item.title: Images Team
item.content:encoded:
Like Vince said this photo is infringing on copyright simply by being identifiable. I'm removing the image code.

I created a CC compliant image that fits the concept. If you want you can use it.

Alternate Image
– hide block

Otherwise you're going to have to find something else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted because I felt the dialogue in the interview log and postlude was kind of weak.

Unrelated, I believe (with about 99% certainty) that the image used in the article is a cropped version of the photo used in this album art. I'd recommend finding a better replacement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing in the main article had me lingeringly aching to downvote. Then read experiment log and it saved it all

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted.

But you still have the word "memetic" in there in a few places, and it's not memetic… it's just psychic/magic/telephathic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Deserves more up upvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, I don't mind the concept but I agree with others - I don't think this effect is memetic, and the main text should be adjusted to remove references to that. Also I find the comments by the Dr unprofessional and jarring; if they are required (and I don't think they are) they could be better intergrated into the tex.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You really didn't? My thoughts about Document 1026-B:

its body lost its anomalous properties

Isn't it too hasty?

The sac is full of… fog

a fog seems like a great way of relocating the creature's essence

the "fog" began glowing

entering active phase

Dr. ███████: Just a minute… I've got a headache all of the sudden…
*12-second gap*
Dr. ███████: Jesus Christ, this hurts… What the-?

I'm completely dumb if this bit doesn't imply anything about possesing Dr Blackbox

The log was ended due to an unexpected loss of power in the autopsy room

A great coverup for something.

When a security team entered, they saw no sign that an autopsy had taken place.

Because it was in the doctor's body by then

Dr. ███████ claimed to have no memory of what had happened.

Well played 1026, well played.

A subsequent autopsy revealed that SCP-1026-B possessed normal human physiology and no anomalous properties whatsoever.

Because it was in the doctor's body by then (2)

Summing my thoughts up: If you really didn't mean to suggest such a possibility you've put a lot of hints to this unintentionally and it's entirely a coincidence. I still can't believe it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I'll leave that one open-ended. However, it did not transfer into the doctor's body.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, that is what that document meant?
I was wondering….
I was thinking that that was its "death cry"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After reading the additional document one thing bugs me:

If we, as casual readers, can see the possibility that this scp is now in the doctor's body how did the Foundation miss this? I would expect them acting a little bit more carefull when a scp has a documented skill of possible body switching. After the autopsy i would suggest indicating that the body's DNA was tested and appeared the same as the Doctor's and Maybe-Not-Dr was contained.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought I saw this guy around, but he left the room and I can't find him anywhere.

Now he's just somebody that I used to know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, +40 rating! Thanks for all the support!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Will do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As per Understanding Memetics, this SCP does not exhibit an actual memetic effect; it is simply a mind-affecting entity that causes you to see it as someone you recognize. Original author, please correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just stumbled across this one and I very much like it. It sounds, from the comments, that it has changed a lot from the original version. My favorite aspect is the escape and how it isn't clear how it happened and what killed it. Nice and vague. Great work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea that some folks just plain can't perceive it at all is a good creepy twist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edited to explain what happens when a psychopath sees it. Thanks, Sorts!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Glass doesn't have any affect on 1026's ability, presumably because glass allows light directly through it, whereas a screen just reproduces what it receives.

Humans can't be completely emotionless, but extreme sociopaths don't notice the entity at all and refuse to acknowledge its existence. Non-humans and some humanoid SCPs (such as Able), see its "true" form.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. Good on the author for removing the 'superpower' paragraph because that kind of killed it for me below. Also, I like the means of reproduction.

Seconding the suggestion that the author read Sorts' post and incorporate some of those suggestions explicitly into the article. Would make a decent article even better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted because of the accompanying document. That was awesome and very creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or in this case, you get both!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely agree. While we can all enjoy the occasional D splatfest, it's usually more creative to have the SCP pose a threat by screwing with peoples minds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Oh, look, that's my favorite chair! I thought it was lost! Where did you guys found it?"

I think I'd like to see this change somewhere in that direction. Current revision, while better, still don't grab me much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*takes a shot*

It could be memetic.

If it is memetic, that means that the entity presents some sort of information that the viewer perceives as [insert your loved one here] and then they act accordingly. Although that should also (but not necessarily) mean that photos and videos of the thing would not be blurry, the viewer could still interpret that visual stimuli as "this is a person important to me" — but maybe there's something about the thing that photos and videos don't pick up. That's fine. But that also means that you can really easily tell what this thing actually is by holding up a digital camera and watching the live recording of it instead of watching it directly. It would also probably mean that if it was in a sealed room with a window and the only exposure is looking at it through glass then it's a blurry non-person instead of the person who looks and sounds and smells just like your mother or wife or whatever.

If it is memetic that means the entire effect is based on how people interpret what it is, as opposed to it magically changing its perception in someone's mind. Memes are information, not telepathy.

But that also means that since this thing is not directly affecting people, the effect should not always be the same. A lot of our D-class are sociopaths (so they say) so who is to say that they have ANY loved ones at all? Maybe there's some monster D-class out there who interprets the [insert loved one here] effect by seeing the little girl with the cutest screams? What would an emotionless person with no personal connections see?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, now this is something I can upvote. Breaking out of containment because it's super strong and fast is fairly ho-hum. Breaking out of containment because the response team can't bear to shoot their loved ones is awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow… That's actually a really good idea!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's a simple idea: Make its escape related to the main idea of appearing as someone the witness knows and loves. Since everyone perceives it as someone they know, there are a ton of ways someone would let it out or not realize it escaped until the thing was halfway out of the site.

The problem with making it an overly durable shapeshifter is that it doesn't need to be an overly durable shapeshifter. It's already got a creepy composition and a creepy effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To all those who are worried about SCP-1026's "superpowers":

I had no intention of making this thing any more dangerous; I just wanted to explain how it managed to break containment. Additionally, having it exist *slightly* outside of normal space/time would produce some unusual effects. I'm not trying to emphasize that it could tear your head off; I tried to focus on the fact that this thing could replace anyone. A member of your own family could be one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. It's a classic "meh" idea of shapeshifting, but executed well enough that I enjoyed reading it - it was neither too shallow, nor was it boring. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How is this anything beyond a thing what can fight good plus technobabble and a disguise?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't like it. It's overpowered, very overpowered, and can reproduce with unknown speed. 173 is weaker than this, and yet when it begun to reproduce it lead to XK fairly quickly. And yet it's Euclid.

And the basic idea I don't like too. It was done better in 268. There it's just mentioned in passing and then it's all about an awesome super-thing that exist in a way what we puny mortals can't even comprehend outside of time and space.

A being that exists outside of time and space can time travel and teleport at will. This one can't.

Picture is nice, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed on this, actually. To the writer, check out the "Understanding Memetics" piece in the guide section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I did not vote for the same reason. Unless someone explains to me why it suddenly became Martian Manhunter, I am decidedly undecided

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Isn't this a cognition hazard, rather than memetic? After all, the conviction that the SCP is [whomever] doesn't spread.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1026 has displayed several previously unknown abilities during Incident 1026-1. These abilities included enhanced strength and speed, camouflage (SCP-1026's body turned partially invisible to observers), and limited shape-changing abilities (SCP-1026 managed to fit through a 3-inch wide gap left by a closing door). SCP-1026 also displayed a remarkable resistance to gunfire; surviving personnel reported that bullets appeared to pass through its body without harming it.

This reminds me of a list of super powers on a comic book wiki. Downvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Consider it dropped.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's like the Flash and the Martian Manhunter were forcibly merged-in a good/horrible way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Memetics and needless violence, and yet a good article. Impressive. Drop "royally screwed" and you have my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Then the D-class started to whisper 'mommy mommy' and then I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't like this idea when I first heard it on the forums. But the execution and picture are good.

EDIT: Now that you've added a title, it's good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. It's one of the best examples of "Hey, this isn't that much of a threat. I call Safe." "It's two miles away and running." "FUCK. Euclid it is, then."
